title: Pattern
description: A compiled execution pattern. Encodes intent as reusable decision structure rather than repeatable text.

version.description: Schema version this pattern was compiled against.

pattern.description: Identity block. Uniquely identifies this pattern across runtimes and registries.

id.description: Unique slug. Kebab-case. Used as the run key: `run pattern:<id>`

description.description: One sentence: what this pattern produces and for whom.

tags.description: Taxonomy labels for search and composition.

source.description: Trace back to the interview that produced this pattern.

extends.description: Optional. ID of parent pattern this one inherits from. Enables composition.

intent.description: What the user wants to accomplish. Extracted verbatim from interview, then normalized.

goal.description: Single declarative sentence. The north star for all execution decisions.
goal.example: Produce accurate, readable API documentation from source code.

success_looks_like.description: Concrete observable outcomes. Each item is a falsifiable statement.
example: A junior developer can onboard using only this output

failure_looks_like.description: Anti-goals. Conditions that, if present, mean the pattern failed regardless of other metrics.

scope.description: What is and is not in scope. Reduces ambiguity at execution time.

constraints.description: Hard limits on how execution must behave. Violations are execution errors, not warnings.

latency.description: Response time expectation. Drives runtime scheduling decisions.

retries.description: Max retry attempts per phase before the pattern halts.

human_review.description: Whether a human must approve output before the pattern proceeds or completes.

output_format.description: Expected output MIME type or format identifier.

token_budget.description: Max tokens the runtime may consume across all phases. null = unlimited.

execution_mode.description: How phases relate to each other at execution time.

custom.description: Domain-specific constraints not covered above. Key-value map.

invariants.description: Properties that must remain true across every phase of execution. If any invariant is violated, the runtime halts. These are the non-negotiables extracted from the interview.

description.description: Plain language statement of what must always be true.
description.example: Output never includes internal system paths

memory.description: State management contract. Defines what is remembered, where, and for how long.

strategy.description: stateless: no carry-over between runs. session: within one execution. persistent: survives across runs. compressed: summarized after N tokens.

carry_forward.description: Named state keys that must be passed between phases.

forget_after.description: Duration after which persistent memory expires. ISO 8601 duration.

phases.description: The execution pipeline. Each phase is a discrete unit of work with defined inputs, outputs, and transition logic.

id.description: Short slug used in transition logic.

objective.description: Single sentence: what this phase must accomplish.
objective.example: Parse source code and extract all public endpoints with their signatures.

input.description: What this phase requires to begin.

requires.description: Named keys from memory or prior phase output.

accepts.description: Optional inputs that enrich execution if present.

produces.description: Named keys written to memory for downstream phases.

transitions.description: Where execution goes next, based on outcome.

on_empty.description: Phase ID or action when this phase produces no output.

hint.description: Optional guidance for the runtime on how to approach this phase. Not an instruction prompt — a structural nudge.

evaluation.description: How the runtime decides the pattern succeeded or failed overall.

strategy.description: auto: runtime evaluates against success_looks_like. rubric: scored criteria. human: explicit approval. hybrid: auto then human if threshold not met.

rubric.description: Used when strategy is rubric or hybrid.

minimum_pass_score.description: Weighted score below which the pattern is considered failed.

runtime.description: Execution environment preferences. All fields optional — runtime uses defaults if absent.

preferred_model.description: Suggested model. Runtime may override based on token budget.

temperature.description: 0 = deterministic, 1 = creative. Most patterns should be 0–0.3.

compatible_runtimes.description: Named executors this pattern has been validated against.

export_as_prompt.description: If true, runtime can serialize this pattern back into a prompt string for tools that don't support structured execution.
